demos.prompt: Explain why the sky is blue

graph.prompt: Explain why the sky is blue

nodes.label: Fact
nodes.content: Sunlight contains the full visible spectrum (400–700 nm). Earth’s atmosphere consists mainly of nitrogen and oxygen molecules.

nodes.label: Physics
nodes.content: Rayleigh scattering: shorter wavelengths (blue ≈450 nm) scatter roughly 10× more than longer wavelengths (red ≈700 nm) when photons interact with atmospheric molecules.

nodes.label: Conclusion
nodes.content: The sky appears blue because scattered blue light reaches our eyes from every direction, while red/orange wavelengths travel more directly and only dominate at sunrise/sunset.

demos.conclusion: The sky appears blue because scattered blue light reaches our eyes from every direction, while red/orange wavelengths travel more directly and only dominate at sunrise/sunset.

demos.original_content: Rayleigh scattering: shorter wavelengths (blue ≈450 nm) scatter roughly 10× more than longer wavelengths (red ≈700 nm) when photons interact with atmospheric molecules.
demos.new_content: Because of clouds blocking and scattering light differently
demos.new_conclusion: Clouds scatter and absorb light differently than gas molecules. When clouds dominate the sky, they block and diffuse light, making the sky appear white or grey rather than blue.

graph.prompt: Explain why the sky is blue

nodes.label: Fact
nodes.content: Sunlight contains the full visible spectrum (400–700 nm). Earth’s atmosphere consists mainly of nitrogen and oxygen molecules.

nodes.label: Physics
nodes.content: Because of clouds blocking and scattering light differently

nodes.label: Conclusion
nodes.content: Clouds scatter and absorb light differently than gas molecules. When clouds dominate the sky, they block and diffuse light, making the sky appear white or grey rather than blue.

demos.conclusion: Clouds scatter and absorb light differently than gas molecules. When clouds dominate the sky, they block and diffuse light, making the sky appear white or grey rather than blue.

nodes.label: Setup
nodes.content: Identify the operation: percentage calculation requires multiplying the base by (percent / 100).

nodes.label: Computation
nodes.content: Apply the formula: value × (percent ÷ 100). Follow standard order of operations.

nodes.label: Answer
nodes.content: The computation yields the precise numerical result according to the formula.

demos.conclusion: The computation yields the precise numerical result according to the formula.
